raw_consumer_statement: I noticed a recurring transfer on my acct that I did not recognize on XX/XX/XXXX and reached out to American Express to dispute the transactions- the merchant had been charging me monthly since XX/XX/XXXX. They requested that I reach out to the merchant, and refused to open the dispute without me having done so. This is in violation of Reg Z, which does not require a consumer to reach out to the merchant for a billing dispute. When I separately reached out to the merchant ( because they refused to open the dispute ), the merchant did not pick up. I called AmEx to open the dispute and they tried to validate the merchant number I called and give me a different number for the merchant for me to call. Step after step they were blocking me from opening a dispute. Further, they then told me I was only eligible to dispute the most recent transaction. Again, another Reg Z violation because the rule is that you can dispute transactions 60 days from delivery of the statement, so I had at least 3 transactions eligible for dispute. I had to point this out to the agent, who got visibility frustrated and kept cutting me off ( trying to follow her incorrect procedures ). Only after I threatened the CFPB complaint did I get her to open a dispute on the 3 most recent transactions. She wouldn't even log my notice of error for the older transactions ( even if the outcome would have been non-payment/non-investigation by AmEx ). Overall, a poor experience and made me reconsider the long standing relationship I have with them.
